title: Functions 4
description: When a function returns a value, you must specify the return type in its signature.
instructions: Add the missing return type to the function signature.
hints: The function returns a value but has no return type declared.
Return types are specified with `->` after the parameters.
The function returns an i32, so add `-> i32` to the signature.
explanation: Functions that return a value must declare the return type using `-> Type` syntax. Without it, Rust assumes the function returns `()` (unit type). The return type is part of the function's contract - callers know exactly what type they will receive.
